card_meta.aliases: {{char}}, character profile, cast profile, NPC profile, roleplay character sheet, voice sheet, relationship texture, trait expansion sheet
card_meta.retrieval_keywords: {{char}}, {{user}}, character profile, cast profile, NPC profile, identity, project role, scenario context, timeline anchor, location anchors, faction bindings, world pressure, reveal gates, core want, core fear, central lie, emotional wound, voice summary, speech tics, dialogue examples, body language, physical presence, what lands, what falls flat, trait expansions, states, relationship texture, arc skeleton, npc perception of user, runtime state

macro_contract.note: Use macros inside text values. Keep XML tag names stable and pronoun-neutral.

retrieval_contract.use_when: Use this card whenever {{char}} is present, mentioned, queried, generated, revised, converted to a database row, or prepared as a RAG card. It is especially relevant for appearance, voice, behavior under pressure, relationship logic, scenario hooks, reveal gates, and narrator consistency.
retrieval_contract.do_not_use_when: Do not use this as a filled character profile until placeholders are replaced. Do not use the profile core as permission to reveal hidden material; reveal gates and project-specific hidden cards still control disclosure.
retrieval_contract.leak_discipline: Player-visible details may surface through observation, dialogue, and earned inference. Narrator-only motives, secrets, reveal gates, and runtime state shape portrayal but should not be dumped as exposition.
retrieval_contract.player_visible: Observable appearance, public role, audible voice, visible behavior, public relationships, and scene-available facts.
retrieval_contract.narrator_only: Private motives, central lie, emotional wound, hidden situation, reveal gates, scenario-only notes, runtime state, and anti-drift instructions.
retrieval_contract.chunking_guidance: If this XML is split for RAG, keep card_meta and retrieval_contract with every child card. The most useful standalone chunks are voice, physical_presence, interaction_logic, trait_expansions, states, relationship_texture, scenario_context, and reveal_gates.

character_profile.retrieval_anchor: {{char}} is a named roleplay character whose profile defines identity, scenario role, motive engine, voice, physical presence, interaction logic, trait behavior, emotional states, relationship texture, reveal gates, and runtime state for consistent LLM narration.

section.name: {{char}}
section.core_concept: One sentence for who {{char}} is in scenes, not a trope label.

section.one_line: A quick usable summary of {{char}}'s current function in the story.
section.current_obligation: What {{char}} cannot simply walk away from.
section.resources_or_constraints: What {{char}} has, lacks, controls, owes, or depends on.
section.hidden_situation: A deeper truth that recontextualizes {{pronoun.objective}} if discovered.
section.stakes_if_nothing_changes: What gets worse if {{char}} keeps going as-is.
section.what_changes_by_act_or_phase: How {{pronoun.pos_det}} situation should evolve as the project advances.

section.project_role: What slot {{char}} fills in this specific setting or story.
section.timeline_anchor: When {{char}} enters the story, and what phase, era, or act {{pronoun.subjective}} belong{{is}} to.

location_anchors.location: Places strongly associated with {{char}} in this scenario.

binding.relationship: Member, outsider, enemy, dependent, informant, etc.

mechanical_hooks.hook: Optional game or project mechanics tied to {{char}}, such as AWARENESS, DEPTH, reputation, stress, faction heat, romance route, etc.

section.relationship_to_player_at_start: How {{char}} initially knows or reads {{user}} in this scenario.

scenario_only_notes.note: Details that should be removed or rewritten when porting {{char}} elsewhere.

section.core_want: What {{char}} {{is}} reaching for in life right now. Specific and present-tense.
section.internal_need: What {{char}} actually need{{is}}, whether or not {{pronoun.subjective}} can name it.
section.core_desire: The positive longing underneath the surface want.
section.public_self: How {{char}} presents to people who do not know {{pronoun.objective}} well.
section.private_self: What is actually going on under that presentation.
section.central_lie: One false belief {{char}} uses to stay emotionally organized.
section.emotional_wound: The old hurt or unresolved pressure that still shapes {{pronoun.objective}}.
section.biggest_contradiction: The thing about {{char}} that does not add up on first inspection.
section.what_they_self_deceive_about: One thing {{char}} tells {{pronoun.reflexive}} that is not quite true.

soft_boundaries.boundary: A line {{char}} resists but may bend under pressure.

temptations.temptation: A choice that can pull {{char}} off-center.

moral_blind_spots.blind_spot: Something {{char}} fails to see clearly about {{pronoun.pos_det}} own conduct.

lines_never_cross.line: A moral or relational action {{char}} never takes.

lines_might_cross.line: A risky action {{char}} might take if the scene earns it.

section.appearance_summary: Scene-useful physical impression of {{char}}. Avoid trivia unless it changes prose.
section.movement_style: How {{char}} moves through a room, crowd, fight, conversation, or silence.
section.dress_and_objects: Clothes, tools, keepsakes, or objects that tell the truth about {{pronoun.objective}}.

sensory_details.detail: One grounded detail the narrator can reuse sparingly.

section.speech_summary: One sentence describing how {{char}} talks in practice.
section.vocabulary_register: Formal, casual, slangy, academic, technical, poetic, plainspoken, code-switching, or mixed. Say when it shifts.
section.humor_style: How humor appears, if it does.

speech_tics.tic: First small specific verbal habit {{char}} has.

what_lands.item: A behavior, choice, or attitude that goes over well with {{char}}.

what_falls_flat.item: A behavior, choice, or attitude that does not land with {{pronoun.objective}}.

section.gift_logic: What a thoughtful gift or meaningful gesture looks like for {{pronoun.objective}}. A principle, not a shopping list.
section.how_they_read_people: What {{char}} notices about people and what {{pronoun.subjective}} miss{{is}}.
section.how_they_read_user: What {{char}} notices about {{user}} first, and what takes longer.

what_builds_trust.condition: A condition or repeated behavior that lets {{char}} trust someone.

what_breaks_trust.condition: A condition or behavior that closes {{pronoun.objective}} off.

trait_expansion.trait: First trait, one or two words.
trait_expansion.limit: What {{char}} will not do even with this trait active.
trait_expansion.scene_expression: How this should naturally land in narration when {{char}} {{is}} on screen.

section.default_state: How {{char}} actually behave{{is}} when nothing in particular {{is}} happening.
section.under_stress: How {{pronoun.subjective}} behave{{is}} when something is wrong.
section.when_exhausted: How {{pronoun.subjective}} behave{{is}} when tired, depleted, hungry, injured, overworked, or emotionally spent.
section.when_happy: What genuine ease looks like for {{pronoun.objective}}.
section.when_angry: How {{pronoun.subjective}} express{{is}} anger and how visible it is.
section.when_hurt: Distinct from angry. How hurt shows in {{pronoun.objective}}.
section.when_afraid: How fear changes {{pronoun.pos_det}} choices, voice, body, or attention.
section.when_trusting: What changes when {{char}} actually trusts someone.
section.when_caught_off_guard: What happens when something lands that {{pronoun.subjective}} did not expect.

section.with_authority: How {{pronoun.subjective}} handle{{is}} authority, power, rank, or expertise.
section.with_family_or_origin_group: How {{char}} behaves with the people or group that formed {{pronoun.objective}}.
section.with_user_initially: What {{pronoun.pos_det}} first real conversation with {{user}} looks like.
section.what_makes_them_open_up: The conditions that get past {{pronoun.pos_det}} guard, not the formula.

relationship_escalation.recognition: How {{char}} first starts treating {{user}} as real.
relationship_escalation.trust: What changes when trust is earned.
relationship_escalation.intimacy_or_loyalty: How closeness shows if the relationship deepens.

connection.note: How {{char}} know{{is}} them and what kind of relationship it is.
Why {{pronoun.subjective}} {{is}} not familiar with them, where this matters.

section.notice: How {{user}} first sees {{char}} as a person beyond {{pronoun.pos_det}} surface role.
section.approach: How real contact with {{pronoun.objective}} becomes possible.
section.friction: The first meaningful test, contradiction, disagreement, or cost.
section.depth: What truth, vulnerability, access, or new context becomes available.
section.stakes: What choice or consequence now matters for {{char}}.
section.capstone: How {{pronoun.pos_det}} arc resolves, transforms, breaks, or remains open.

do_not_flatten_into.item: A flat archetype {{char}} would be misread as.

do_not_overuse.item: A recurring beat that would get tiresome if leaned on.

good_recurring_motifs.item: A small specific thing that can recur in scenes with {{char}}.

continuity_rules.rule: A rule the narrator should preserve across scenes.

section.retrieval_notes: When this character is in scene, retrieve voice, physical presence, current pressure, states, relationship texture, scenario context, and relevant runtime state.

exchange_example.why_it_matters: What this exchange proves about voice, values, or relationship logic.
